title: Weight Filtration as Depth Functor

analysis.object: T699: Weight Filtration as Depth Functor depth analysis

weight_functor.description: Weight filtration W_k is a functor from MHS to filtered objects; Deltad=k/2 at each stage
weight_functor.reason: Formalized: W_k is a depth-k/2 functor; every Hodge structural theorem is now an EML theorem

transfers_free.description: Transfer theorem: every EML structural result transfers to Hodge via weight=depth identification
transfers_free.reason: Free transfers: tropical lemmas, shadow depth theorem, no-inverse lemma all apply to Hodge theory

depth_preserving_morphisms.description: Morphisms of Hodge structures are depth-preserving functors (Deltad=0)
depth_preserving_morphisms.reason: Hodge morphisms: depth-preserving by construction; weight filtration respected implies depth respected

algebraicity_constraint.description: Algebraic cycles are depth-0 objects; Hodge classes are depth-k/2; bijection = depth bridge
algebraicity_constraint.reason: Hodge bijection rephrased: depth-0 algebraic cycles <-> depth-k/2 Hodge classes; depth bridge

analysis.theorem: T699: Weight Filtration as Depth Functor (S978).

key_theorem: T699: Weight Filtration as Depth Functor (S978).
